title: epacris microphylla var. gunnii
intro: Epacris microphylla var. gunnii, also known as the Alpine Heath, is a low-growing, evergreen shrub native to Tasmania and southern Australia. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, making it useful in treating wounds and skin conditions. The Alpine Heath has been traditionally used to promote respiratory health, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, offering a range of health benefits. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves can be dried or infused in hot water to create a tea or tincture, which can be used to support overall well-being.
